strengths: Proven experience building and operating event-driven, distributed systems with real-time data processing — critical for agentic AI and fintech infrastructure. Work at Sociomantic (Dunnhumby media) involved handling millions of HTTP requests per second using low-latency microservices, demonstrating deep backend and distributed-systems expertise.

Direct contributions to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling, including Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows at re:cinq. This aligns closely with the need for modular, stateful, tool-augmented agents in the CTO role.

Strong track record of technical leadership, engineering discipline, and cross-functional collaboration across fintech, data, and real-time systems. Experience scaling teams, implementing CI/CD, observability, and governance frameworks (e.g., at Altom Consulting, RIDE Capital, and Optilyz) supports the engineering leadership responsibilities.
concerns: No explicit executive-level leadership experience as a CTO, VP, or Head of Engineering — senior roles are primarily hands-on or staff-level, not full organizational ownership.
Limited direct experience in regulated fintech environments with credit risk, compliance, or auditability infrastructure — most fintech exposure is in pricing and investment, not core infrastructure.
Career gap and short tenure at re:cinq (6 months) may raise questions about stability and long-term commitment to a high-stakes scale-up role.

key_factors.factor: Agentic AI and distributed systems experience

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo presents a compelling fit for the CTO role due to his deep technical expertise in event-driven, real-time distributed systems and direct involvement in AI-agent tooling and orchestration. His background in high-scale data infrastructure (Sociomantic), fintech (Optilyz, RIDE Capital), and engineering leadership (Altom, re:cinq) demonstrates the architectural fluency and production rigor required for agentic AI in regulated fintech. While his executive experience is limited, his hands-on leadership in scaling teams, implementing observability, and governing hybrid engineering models signals strong upside in transitioning to full ownership of technical vision and organizational growth.
